Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). This introduced several new resource types (`DevicePoolResource`, `EdgeMachineResource`, `ClusterJobResource`, `EdgeMachineJobResource`, `OsImageResource`, `PlatformUpdateResource`, `UpdateContentResource`, `ValidatedSolutionRecipeResource`) and updated property shapes throughout the library (e.g. `ConnectivityProperties` changed from `BinaryData` to a typed model).\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **Wrong service description**: The README opening paragraph reads *\"Microsoft Azure VMware Solution is a VMware validated solution with ongoing validation and testing of enhancements and upgrades…\"* — this copy was incorrectly pasted from another package and has nothing to do with Azure Stack HCI.\n- **No Examples for new resource types**: The migration added 8 new resource types (`DevicePoolResource`, `EdgeMachineResource`, `ClusterJobResource`, `EdgeMachineJobResource`, `OsImageResource`, `PlatformUpdateResource`, `UpdateContentResource`, `ValidatedSolutionRecipeResource`) but the README `## Examples` section contains no samples for any of them.\n- **Generated samples not created for new types**: `tests/Generated/Samples/` has sample files for pre-existing types but none for the new types from this migration; snippet placeholders in the README cannot be wired up until these sample files exist.\n- **CHANGELOG entries are vague**: The `1.3.0-beta.1` entry says \"Added support for new resource types and operations from the latest API version\" without naming any of them — each new resource type should be called out explicitly.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n#### Fix the service description in the README\n\n**File:** `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci/README.md` — **edit**\n\nReplace the incorrect opening paragraph (lines after the `# Microsoft Azure Stack HCI management client library for .NET` heading):\n\n```markdown\nMicrosoft Azure VMware Solution is a VMware validated solution with ongoing validation and testing of enhancements and upgrades. Microsoft manages and maintains the private cloud infrastructure and software. It allows you to focus on developing and running workloads in your private clouds to deliver business value.\n\nThis library supports managing Microsoft Azure Stack HCI resources.\n```\n\nwith:\n\n```markdown\nAzure Stack HCI is a hyperconverged infrastructure (HCI) solution that lets you run virtualized Windows and Linux workloads in your on-premises environment while connecting to Azure for cloud services. This library supports managing Azure Stack HCI resources such as clusters, arc extensions, edge devices, device pools, OS images, and more.\n```\n\n#### Fix the CHANGELOG to name the new resource types\n\n**File:** `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci/CHANGELOG.md` — **edit**\n\nReplace the vague `Features Added` bullet:\n\n```markdown\n- Added support for new resource types and operations from the latest API version\n```\n\nwith:\n\n```markdown\n- Added support for new resource types introduced in API version `2026-04-01-preview`:\n  - `DevicePoolResource` / `DevicePoolCollection`\n  - `EdgeMachineResource` / `EdgeMachineCollection`\n  - `ClusterJobResource` / `ClusterJobCollection`\n  - `EdgeMachineJobResource` / `EdgeMachineJobCollection`\n  - `OsImageResource` / `OsImageCollection`\n  - `PlatformUpdateResource` / `PlatformUpdateCollection`\n  - `UpdateContentResource` / `UpdateContentCollection`\n  - `ValidatedSolutionRecipeResource` / `ValidatedSolutionRecipeCollection`\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nFor each new resource type below, create a snippet test file and wire a placeholder into the README.\n\n#### Snippet: DevicePool management\n\n1. **Create** `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci/tests/Samples/Sample_DevicePool.cs`\n\n```csharp\nusing System;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing Azure.Identity;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Hci;\nusing NUnit.Framework;\n\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Tests.Samples\n{\n    public class Sample_DevicePool\n    {\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n        public async Task GetDevicePool()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:Hci_GetDevicePool\n            string subscriptionId = \"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\";\n            string resourceGroupName = \"hcicluster-rg\";\n            string clusterName = \"HCICluster\";\n            string devicePoolName = \"myDevicePool\";\n\n            var cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\n            var client = new ArmClient(cred);\n\n            ResourceIdentifier devicePoolId = DevicePoolResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(\n                subscriptionId, resourceGroupName, clusterName, devicePoolName);\n            DevicePoolResource devicePool = client.GetDevicePoolResource(devicePoolId);\n\n            DevicePoolResource result = await devicePool.GetAsync();\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Device pool name: {result.Data.Name}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n2. **Edit** `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci/README.md` — add under `## Examples`, create a new subsection `### Device Pool Management`:\n\n```markdown\n#### Get a Device Pool\n\n```C# Snippet:Hci_GetDevicePool\n```\n```\n\n#### Snippet: OS Image management\n\n1. **Create** OsImageResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(\n                subscriptionId, resourceGroupName, clusterName, osImageName);\n            OsImageResource osImage =
